refnamediv.refpurpose: Sign system reports with a local software key

refsect1.title: Description
refsect1.para: systemd-report-sign-plain@.service is a system service that signs system reports generated by systemd-report1. It is a signing backend for the --sign logic of that tool: it implements the io.systemd.Report.Sign.Sign() Varlink method and is reached via a socket linked into the /run/systemd/report.sign/ directory, named plain.

The service is socket-activated (one instance per connection) via systemd-report-sign-plain.socket, and signs the digest passed to it with a local software key. Signatures are made with the Ed25519 (EdDSA) algorithm.

On first use a key pair is generated automatically and stored below /var/lib/systemd/report.sign.plain/: the private key in local.private (PKCS#8 PEM, readable only by the root user) and the public key in local.public (PEM, world readable). An existing private key is never overwritten, even if it cannot be loaded: in that case the service fails instead, so that a misconfiguration does not silently invalidate previously made signatures.

The signature reply consists of the raw Ed25519 signature (Base64 encoded) together with the public key it was made with (in PEM form), so that signatures can be verified without prior knowledge of the key.
